CHAPTER 113
(Н. В. 503)
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
( 1 ) Except as otherwise provided in section ( 14 ) the
sheriff of each county shall annually assess every vehicle that is
subject to ad valorem taxation which is required to be registered
under Chapter 186 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes and motor
vehicles taxed under KRS 281.811 , except those vehicles now
assessed under Chapter 136 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
The sheriff shall prepare a tax bill for each vehicle assessed . The
tax bill shall show the assessed valuation , the amount of tax due
the state , county and other taxing districts , the total , and such
other information as the Department of Revenue deems neces-
sary.
( 2 ) For his assessment services and preparation of each
tax bill , the sheriff shall be allowed in addition to the compensa-
tion set forth in KRS 134.290 the sum of ten cents . One-half of
this sum shall be paid by the state and one -half shall be paid by
the county .
( 3 ) The Department of Revenue shall annually furnish to
each sheriff sufficient tax bills and other necessary forms to ad-
minister this Act , and shall , by regulation , prescribe the ac-
counting control of tax bills and moneys collected .
(4) The county court clerk shall not register any vehicle
until the owner has presented a tax receipt showing that the ad
valorem tax on such vehicle has been paid or a statement pre-
pared by the sheriff that the vehicle was not subject to ad
valorem tax as of January 1. The Department of Revenue shall
by regulation prescribe for the disposition of the tax receipt fur-
nished to the county court clerk .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1956, p. 226

classification.title: AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation.
classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky law regarding the annual assessment and taxation of vehicles. It requires sheriffs to assess vehicles subject to ad valorem taxation and motor vehicles taxed under KRS 281.811, excluding those already assessed under KRS 136. The sheriff will prepare tax bills detailing the assessed valuation and taxes due to the state, county, and other taxing districts. Sheriffs are compensated for these services. The Department of Revenue will provide necessary forms and prescribe accounting controls. County court clerks are prohibited from registering vehicles without proof of ad valorem tax payment or a statement from the sheriff indicating the vehicle was not subject to tax as of January 1.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the assessment and taxation of vehicles. It outlines procedures for sheriffs and county court clerks regarding tax collection and vehicle registration. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

(5) The Department of Motor Transportation shall not

a tax receipt showing that the ad valorem tax on each vehicle has

(6) Any owner of any vehicle who does not agree with the
assessed valuation placed upon his vehicle or vehicles by the
sheriff shall have the right to appeal such assessment to the
board of supervisors which convenes as set out in KRS 133.030 ,
or as provided by other taxing jurisdictions involved . The ap-
peal shall be filed and treated as appeals on other property . An
owner of a vehicle who appeals the assessment on his vehicle
shall pay to the sheriff the tax due on the contested as well as
the uncontested portion of the assessment . The sheriff shall hold
in a safe depository the tax paid on the contested portion . If the
final review authority as set out in KRS 133.120 , or as provided
by other taxing jurisdictions involved , decreases the assessed
valuation as determined by the sheriff , the sheriff shall cause to
be refunded to the owner that portion of the tax paid over and
above the correct amount . If the final review authority sustains
the assessed valuation as originally determined by the sheriff , the
sheriff shall pay over to the state , county , and other taxing dis-
tricts for whom the taxes were collected the taxes withheld on
the contested portion of the assessed valuation . All taxes col-
lected on vehicles by the sheriff shall be paid and reported to the
various taxing districts as other ad valorem taxes are paid and
reported .
(7) Every corporation , company , association, partnership ,
or person whose property is centrally assessed under Chapter 136
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes and who owns a motor vehicle
subject to ad valorem taxes shall assess and pay the taxes as now
provided by Chapter 136 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes . The
county court clerk shall not register any vehicle or the Depart-
ment of Motor Transportation shall not issue a permit required by
Kentucky Revised Statutes 281.811 , 281.812 or 281.815 ( 1 ) and
( 3 ) upon a vehicle until the owner has presented a receipt
from the Department of Revenue showing the ad valorem tax has
been paid on each vehicle , or an indemnity bond posted with the
Department of Revenue to cover such taxes due , or that no tax
was due .

CHAPTER 113 207
(8) It is the intent of the legislature that motor vehicles
assessable under this Act not be assessed for taxes by the county
tax commissioners as of January 1, 1957, but that motor vehicles
be assessed and the taxes paid as set forth in this Act based on
ownership of such vehicles as of January 1, 1958. The applicable
rate shall be the rate in effect as of July 1, preceding the assess-
ment date for other tangible property not carrying a special rate .
The ad valorem taxes on such motor vehicles will be received
by the state, county , and other taxing districts in the same fiscal
year as ad valorem taxes on other property which was assessed
as of January 1, 1957 , or December 31 , 1956 .
(9) The owner who wants to register his vehicle before
January 1 for the succeeding year shall assess and pay the ad
valorem taxes due thereon and shall be deemed the owner of the
vehicle as of the following January 1. Any taxes collected prior
to the January 1 assessment date shall be held in escrow by the
sheriff until his January report is made. If the vehicle which
has been registered and the tax paid prior to January 1 is de-
stroyed or damaged prior to January 1, the owner thereof must
file a claim for refund of taxes from the sheriff on or before
January 15. Such claims for refund shall be reviewed as provided
in section 6 of this Act.
(10) Any city or other taxing district may contract with
the sheriff for assessment and collection of the ad valorem taxes
due on vehicles under this Act . The sheriff shall not receive
more than four percent commission for his services for collecting
and reporting funds due any such taxing district . The com-
pensation of the sheriff for collecting all taxes shall not exceed
that which is authorized in KRS 134.290 ( 3 ) .
( 11 ) Section 132.590 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1) In the calendar year 1957 and thereafter , the compen-
sation of the county tax commissioner shall be computed on the
basis of ten cents on each one hundred dollars of the first two and
one -half million dollars and two cents on each one hundred dol-
lars of the excess over two and one-half million dollars of the
annual assessment . However , each county tax commissioner
shall receive a minimum monthly payment of two hundred dol-
lars as part or all of his compensation .

classification.title: Amendment to KRS 132.590 regarding motor vehicle assessment and taxation
classification.summary: This statute amends Kentucky Revised Statutes Section 132.590 to change the assessment and taxation procedures for motor vehicles. It shifts the assessment date for motor vehicles from January 1, 1957, to January 1, 1958, and outlines procedures for early registration, tax payment, and refunds. It also details the sheriff's commission for collecting these taxes and amends the compensation structure for county tax commissioners.

the county clerk shall be allowed seventeen cents for each tax

( 12) ( a ) Section 134.290 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) For the calendar year 1957 , the sheriff shall be allowed
by the Department of Revenue for collecting state taxes , a com-
mission of ten percent upon the first ten thousand dollars and
four percent upon the residue collected or accounted for and paid
into the State Treasury for each calendar year . For the calendar
year 1958 and each year thereafter the sheriff shall be allowed
by the Department of Revenue , for collecting state taxes , a com-
mission of ten percent upon the first five thousand dollars and
four percent upon the residue collected or accounted for and
paid into the State Treasury for each calendar year .
( 2 ) He shall be allowed by the county treasurer , for col-
lecting the county revenue , ten percent upon the first five
thousand dollars collected , and four percent upon the residue.

seventy-five thousand , the aggregate annual compensation of
the sheriff for official services shall not exceed seven thousand
two hundred dollars , independent of the compensation of legally
authorized deputies and assistants and reasonable expenses as
limited by the fiscal court .

sary to the efficient operation of the department . When the
numbers are so decreased, the youngest in point of service in
each particular job classification shall be the first to be re-
leased and shall be placed at the head of the eligible list in
the order of their seniority .

CHAPTER 115 221
Section 10. Section 150.170 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1) Except as provided in subsections ( 2 ) , ( 3 ) , ( 4 ) , ( 5 )
and ( 6 ) of this section, and subject to regulations adopted
under this chapter , no person shall do any act authorized by
any kind of license or permit , or assist in any way any person
in doing any act provided for in this chapter with respect to
game and fish unless he holds the kind of license or permit
that authorizes such act . It is the specific purpose of this
chapter to prohibit the taking or pursuing of any wild life or
wild animals , protected or unprotected , or the angling in any
stream or body of water , whether public or private , without
first procuring the license provided for herein , except to such
extent as may be otherwise provided in this section .
(2) A person under sixteen years of age may, without a
fishing license , take fish by angling , or take minnows by the
use of a minnow seine .
( 3 ) The resident owner of farmlands , his wife , or children ,
or tenants and their children , residing upon said farmland
shall , without procuring license, have the right to take fish
or hunt during the open season ( except trapping ) on said farm-
lands of which they are bona fide owners , or wife or children
of such owners , or tenants or children of such tenants .
(4) Residents or non -residents attending and participat-
ing in any annual state or national field trial may so attend and
participate without obtaining a hunting license .
(5) Any resident of this state who is sixty-five years of
age or older may, without a hunting or fishing license , do any
act authorized by such a license , but while so doing he must
carry upon his person an affidavit stating the year and place
of his birth .
(6 ) The provisions of this chapter for the protection of
wild animals shall not apply to resident landowners of this
state who kill on their land any wild animal causing damage
to such land or any personal property situated thereon. Upon
destruction of any animal or bird by a landowner such act
must be reported to the Department of Fish and Wild Life
Resources or the resident conservation officer for the proper
disposition of the carcass .

dead , transport the same to this state, as part of his personal
baggage at any time during the open season for the particular
species in the state in which taken or within ten days after
the close of such season . Any person may ship dead game
or dead fish so taken subject to the provisions of the laws of
the state in which the same was taken and the laws and regu-
lations of the United States relating thereto .
(7) No person shall import or transport into the state
of Kentucky or receive shipment in the state from without the
state , any type or species of live game, live wild animal , live
wild bird or fish , live amphibian , protected or unprotected
under this chapter , excepting minnows to be used for bait , with-
out first procuring from the department a written permit to
do so . If it is determined upon inspection that such game , wild
animals , wild birds , fish or amphibians would not constitute a
menace to the state and are free from disease that might spread
throughout the state to detriment of wild life resources , the
commissioner shall issue such a permit free of charge .
(8 ) Any carcass of a coon or muskrat from which the
fur has been removed and taken in open season may be legally
sold by the taker only within 90 days after the season closes .
( 9 ) This section shall not in any way interfere with the
transportation of any wild animals , wild birds or fish by the
authorized officers of the department .
( 10 ) Federal regulations shall govern the transportation
of
allmigratory wild birds

Section 17. Section 150.390 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) No person shall take , pursue , or attempt to take or

unless the commissioner , with the approval of the commission ,
finds conditions and population that warrant an open season , then
regulations shall be enacted to govern same .

a spot light , head light, or other artificial lighting device on any

which such deer or rabbit could be killed , even though such
game be not shot at, injured or killed.
(3) It shall be unlawful for any dog to chase or molest in

other land . Any conservation officer , peace officer , sheriff , or
constable may take necessary steps to stop, prevent , or bring
under control any dog , or dogs , found chasing or molesting deer
at any time .
(4) It shall be unlawful to shoot or attempt to shoot any
wild birds or wild animals , protected or unprotected , or any fur
bearing animals from an automobile , or other vehicle , at any
time or place .

Section 19. Section 150.460 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) No person , firm or corporation , whether acting in a
private or public capacity , shall place or cause to be placed in any
public waters any substance that might injure , interfere with or
cause the waters to be unfit for the support of aquatic life. When
any employe of a person , firm or corporation , in the course of his
employment , places or causes to be placed any such substance
in any public waters , his act shall be prima facie evidence of the
guilt of both the employe and employer , and either one or both
may be punished as provided in paragraph ( a ) of subsection (4 )
of KRS 150.990 . Each day that this section is violated shall
constitute a separate offense. This section does not apply to
employes of the department acting in official capacity .
(2 ) No person shall use , or attempt to use any electrical
device of any type ( such as telephone , or electrical leads from
a magneto , or battery , or motor or any other type of electrical
equipment ) in any stream or body of water . This does not pre-
vent the authorized employes of the department from using this
or any devices in research or investigational work .
(3 ) No person shall willfully place or attempt to place
in any of the public waters of the state any substance which has
a poisonous effect or intoxicating effect upon aquatic life .
(4 ) Conservation officers and other persons appointed by
the commissioner and all peace officers have the authority to
enforce KRS 220.580 to 220.990 , known as the Water Pollution
Control Law .
Section 20. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
No person shall violate any law or regulations relating to
licensed shooting preserves , or licensed hunting preserves . Any
owner or operator who permits any person to hunt on his or her
licensed shooting preserve without the required license may have
his or her license cancelled or revoked by the Commissioner of
the Department of Fish and Wild Life Resources .
Section 21. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
No person engaged in hunting or fishing upon either public
or private property shall commit any acts of depredation , such

is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The commissioner , with the approval of the commis-

tory waterfowl thereon shall make an application and pay an

build and construct blinds or pits on said land or water in con-
formance with the laws and regulations of this department .

after the last day of the season during which it shall be lawful to

(5) The holder of such permits shall keep a daily register
and kill survey as set out in the regulation .

of land and water , or land , or water , shall be required to secure

It outlines separate offenses for each bird, fish, or animal taken, possessed, bought, sold, or transported contrary to provisions, and for each device used or possessed illegally. It also includes provisions for license forfeiture for licensees who violate the chapter or regulations.

Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

classification.title: Amendments to Kentucky Revised Statutes Chapter 150 (Fish and Wildlife Resources)
classification.summary: This section of the 1956 Acts of the General Assembly details amendments and repeals to Chapter 150 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, which governs Fish and Wildlife Resources. It outlines penalties for violating specific provisions (KRS 150.185, 150.390, 150.525) and general regulations adopted by the commission. It also repeals KRS 150.221 and introduces a new section allowing the commissioner to issue revenue bonds for public shooting areas, wildlife refuges, and fishing lakes, funded by revenues from these projects.

classification.title: AN ACT relating to the practice of law and the organization of the State Bar.
classification.summary: This act empowers the Court of Appeals to adopt rules governing the practice of law, including defining the practice, establishing a code of ethics, outlining disciplinary procedures, organizing a State Bar association as an administrative agency of the court, requiring membership in good standing for all practicing attorneys, and fixing fees for administration. It also states that these court rules supersede conflicting laws and clarifies that the rules do not prevent individuals from drafting certain legal documents if they are a party to them or not holding themselves out as practicing attorneys.

CHAPTER 117 239
Section 1. Section 392.010 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
All the sections of this chapter , except KRS 392.100 , that re-
late to the wife's dower or interest in the deceased husband's es-
tate, shall apply in all cases , so far as may be, to the husband's
interest in the wife's estate .
Section 2. Section 392.020 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
After the death of the husband or wife intestate, the survivor
shall have an estate in fee of one -half of the surplus real estate of
which the other spouse or anyone for the use of the other spouse ,
was seized of an estate in fee simple at the time of death , and
shall have an estate for his or her life in one -third of any real
estate of which the other spouse or anyone for the use of the
other spouse , was seized of an estate in fee simple during the
coverture but not at the time of death , unless the survivor's
right to such interest has been barred , forfeited or relinquished .
The survivor shall also have an absolute estate in one-half of the
surplus personalty left by the decedent . Unless the context
otherwise requires , any reference in the statutes of this state to
"dower " or "curtesy " shall be deemed to refer to the surviving
spouse's interest created by this section .
Section 3. Section 392.080 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) When a husband or wife dies testate , the surviving
spouse may , though under full age , release what is given to him
or her by will , if any, and receive his or her share under KRS
392.020 as if no will had been made , except that in such case the
share in any real estate of which the decedent or anyone for the
use of the decedent was seized of an estate in fee simple at the
time of death shall be only an estate for the surviving spouse's
life in one-third of such real estate . Such relinquishments shall
be made within twelve months after the probate , and acknowl-
edged before and left for record with the clerk of the court
where probate was made , or acknowledged before a subscribing
witness and proved before and left with the clerk . If, within
those twelve months , an appeal is taken from the judgment pro-
bating the will, the surviving spouse need not make such re
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1956, p. 260

classification.title: An Act to amend KRS 392.010, 392.020, and 392.080 relating to the estates of deceased spouses.
classification.summary: This act amends sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning the inheritance rights of surviving spouses. It aims to equalize the dower and curtesy rights between husbands and wives, ensuring that the surviving spouse receives a specified portion of the deceased spouse's real and personal estate, whether the deceased died testate or intestate. The amendments also clarify how these rights interact with wills and provide a timeframe for relinquishing testamentary gifts to claim statutory inheritance.
classification.reasoning: The statute text provided deals with the inheritance rights of surviving spouses in Kentucky, specifically amending existing statutes related to dower and curtesy. The language used is gender-neutral and focuses on the legal framework of spousal inheritance. There are no explicit or implicit references to race, nor are there any provisions that would suggest discriminatory application based on race. Therefore, this statute is not classified as a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'codes' because it refers to amendments to the 'Kentucky Revised Statutes'.

Section 4. Section 392.050 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

The widow shall be entitled , from the time of the husband's

renunciation , under KRS 392.080 .
Section 5. This Act shall become effective on July 1, 1956.
Approved February 27, 1956

The department is authorized to define terms and amend specifications to align with federal standards.

providing the changes are submitted to the General Assembly

Section 10. ( 1 ) All wholesale egg packs consisting of
cases or portions of cases must bear a label designating contents ,
quality , date of packing , candler's name or number , dealer's
name and address, and size if sold as graded eggs .
(2 ) Graded eggs offered for sale in cartons or other
packs containing one dozen or less shall be plainly marked as to
grade , quality , and size with letters not less than one -fourth inch
in height .
( 3 ) Eggs offered for sale as ungraded candled eggs shall
be plainly marked " Ungraded and Candled " on the cartons with
letters not less than one-fourth inch in height , and in bulk on a
placard with letters not less than one-half inch in height .
Section 11. The commissioner may employ inspectors for
the purpose of enforcing the provisions of the Egg Marketing
Law . Such inspectors may examine any eggs offered or exposed
for sale for human consumption at such times and places and in
such manner as the commissioner may direct . All licensees shall
keep a record of all eggs handled during the license year , and
such other records as the department shall by regulation require .
These records shall be available for examination by authorized
agents of the department .
Section 12. Any person who violates any of the provisions
of the Egg Marketing Law shall be fined an amount not exceed-
ing one hundred dollars for each offense . In a violation involving
sales without a required license , each day of business operation
shall constitute a separate offense .
Section 13. All fees collected under the provisions of the
Egg Marketing Law shall be credited to the department for use
in carrying out the provisions of the Egg Marketing Law .
Section 14. Sections 217.210 , 217.220 , 217.230 , 217.240 ,
and 217.250 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed .
Section 15. This Act shall become effective on the first day
of January, 1957,
Approved February 27, 1956

CHAPTER 122
(Н. В. 151 )
AN ACT relating to forestry .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. There shall be established and maintained within
the nursery or nurseries of the Division of Forestry a section or
sections for the propagation of blight resistant Chinese or Italian
chestnut tree seedlings . Such seedlings shall be distributed
to landowners and citizens of the Commonwealth at reasonable
cost and under and subject to such rules and regulations as may
be established by the Director of Forestry .
Section 2. To carry out the purposes of this Act , there is
appropriated to the Division of Forestry out of the General
Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury the sum of fifteen
thousand dollars for the balance of the 1955-1956 fiscal year . Any
unexpended balance remaining on June 30, 1956 , shall not lapse ,
but shall be available for expenditure during any following fiscal
year .
Approved February 27, 1956

classification.title: An Act relating to motor vehicle safety inspections and repairs.
classification.summary: This act empowers law enforcement officers to inspect motor vehicles for safety and proper equipment. If a vehicle is found unsafe or improperly equipped, the driver will receive a written notice requiring repairs within 48 hours or removal from public highways. It also penalizes repair businesses for falsely certifying completion of repairs and establishes penalties for drivers who fail to comply with the notice. Penalties escalate with repeat offenses.

CHAPTER 126
(Н. В. 213 )
AN ACT relating to the dispensing and sale of narcotic drugs .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 218.060 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) Except as otherwise provided in subsection ( 4 ) of
this section an apothecary , in good faith, may sell and dis-
pense narcotic drugs to any person upon a written prescription
of a physician , dentist , or veterinarian , dated and signed by
the person prescribing on the day when issued and bearing
the full name and address of the patient for whom , or of the
owner of the animal for which , the drug is dispensed , and the
full name , address , and registry number under the federal
narcotic laws of the person prescribing , if he is required by
those laws to be so registered. If the prescription be for an
animal , it shall state the species of animal for which the drug
is prescribed . The person filling the prescription shall write
the date of filling and his own signature on the face of the
prescription . The prescription shall be retained on file by the
proprietor of the pharmacy in which it is filled for a period of
two years , so as to be readily accessible for inspection by any
public officer or employe engaged in the enforcement of Chap-
ter 218 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes. The prescription shall
not be refilled .
( 2) The legal owner of any stock of narcotic drugs in a
pharmacy upon discontinuance of dealing in said drugs , may
sell said stock to a manufacturer , wholesaler , or apothecary , but
only on an official written order .
( 3 ) An apothecary , only upon an official written order ,
may sell to a physician , dentist , or veterinarian , in quantities
not exceeding one ounce at any one time , aqueous or oleaginous
solutions of which the content of narcotic drugs does not exceed
a proportion greater than twenty percent of the complete solu-
tion , to be used for medical purposes .
(4 ) An apothecary may fill an oral prescription com-
municated to him by a physician , dentist , or veterinarian for
such narcotic drugs or compounds of a narcotic drug desig

It also outlines standards for weighing, sampling, and testing equipment and procedures to ensure accuracy and compliance with regulations issued by the director.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text details regulations for the purchase of milk and cream, including licensing for buyers and samplers, and standards for equipment and procedures. There is no mention of race, nor any indication of differential treatment based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by 'ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY' and the year '1956'.

classification.title: An Act relating to the licensing and regulation of milk and cream testers and buyers.
classification.summary: This act establishes licensing requirements for individuals who test, weigh, measure, or sample milk and cream bought from producers, as well as for entities that buy non-packaged milk and cream. It outlines the application process, fees, and grounds for denial or revocation of licenses, and specifies prohibited actions for milk testers.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the regulation of milk and cream testing and buying, establishing licensing requirements for testers and buyers. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial discrimination. The focus is purely on the commercial aspects of the dairy industry and quality control. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is inferred as 'session_laws' because it is presented as a chapter from a legislative session (Gen Assemb 1956.pdf).

Section 7. All persons who weigh , measure or sample milk
or cream bought by buyers from producers shall secure and hold
a license to sample and weigh ; provided , however , that licensed
testers may sample , measure and weigh under the authority of
their license to test. Application for such license shall be made
on a form prescribed by the director and accompanied by the
license fee provided for in section 9 of this Act . Upon receipt of
such application and fee, the director shall issue to the sampler
and weigher a permit to sample and weigh , then after investiga-
tions show him qualified , a license to sample and weigh milk
and cream , provided that the sampler and weigher is in compli-
ance with the provisions of this Act and such regulations relating
to weighing , measuring and sampling of milk and cream as may
be issued by the director ; said license to expire on June 30 of
the license year for which it is issued.
(2 ) All persons who transfer milk from farm bulk tanks
to bulk tank trucks for transporting to a buying location shall
secure and hold a license to sample and weigh .
Section 8. Test equipment including test bottles , pipettes ,
weights , scales and other measuring or calibrating devices used
in testing milk and cream under the provisions of this Act which
are calibrated or adjusted so as to determine the results or ac-
curacy of results of such test shall be inspected by the Experi-
ment Station . Such equipment found accurate shall be stamped ,
sealed or permanently marked so as to be distinguishable . The
charge for initial inspection of test equipment shall be as pre-
scribed by the director but not to exceed 3 cents per piece . Equip-
ment to be stamped for use in Kentucky shall be limited to those
items listed and described on an approved list published period-
ically by the director .

eration after July 1 shall pay 50 cents per month or fraction
thereof for the months remaining in the license year ending June
30.

Section 12. Upon the revocation of any license , the licensee

Section 13. The responsibility for advising the director
concerning enforcement of this Act , for reporting violations , for
conducting investigations and hearing pursuant to recommend-
ing revocation or suspension of a license , and for recommending
rules and regulations to be issued by the director , is vested in an
examining board . The board shall be composed of the head
of the Dairy Section , College of Agriculture and Home Eco-
nomics , University of Kentucky , who shall be chairman , and
three members appointed by the director . Two of three ap-
pointed members shall be appointed from a list of four persons
nominated by the dairy committee of the Kentucky Farm Bu-
reau Federation and the third appointed member shall be ap-
pointed from a list of two persons nominated by the executive
committee of the Dairy Products Association of Kentucky.
The appointed members shall serve for a term of three years and
until their successors are appointed and qualify; except that the
first appointments made under this Act shall be for terms of
one, two , and three years respectively as the director shall desig-
nate . The board shall meet at such time and such place as the
chairman shall designate . Three members present at any meet-
ing shall constitute a quorum , and upon the written request of
any two members , the chairman shall call a meeting at the time
and place requested. Board members not already employed by
the state shall be compensated at the rate of $7.50 per day for

classification.title: Regulation of Kosher Meat Sales
classification.summary: This statute outlines regulations for the sale of kosher and non-kosher meat and meat preparations. It prohibits misrepresentation of meat as kosher, mandates specific signage for establishments selling both types of meat, and defines penalties for violations, including a misdemeanor charge. Possession of non-kosher meat in a place advertising only kosher meat is considered presumptive evidence of intent to defraud.
classification.reasoning: The statute focuses on the regulation of kosher meat sales and the prevention of fraud related to such sales. It does not contain any language that explicitly or implicitly targets any racial group, nor does it establish separate facilities or differential treatment based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is inferred as criminal laws due to the mention of a misdemeanor offense and penalties for violations.

classification.title: AN ACT regulating the traffic in used copper wire and cable.
classification.summary: This act requires dealers of used copper wire and cable to maintain a register of all purchases, including the seller's name and residence, and a description of the purchase. Dealers must permit peace officers to inspect the register and report all purchases to the sheriff (or city police departments in first or second-class cities) within twenty-four hours. Failure to comply is a misdemeanor.
classification.reasoning: The statute, enacted in 1956, regulates the trade of used copper wire and cable. It mandates record-keeping and reporting for dealers to prevent the sale of stolen goods. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that would disproportionately affect a specific racial group. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

266 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 391.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
When a person having right or title to any real estate of
inheritance dies intestate as to such estate , it shall descend
in common to his kindred , male and female , in the following
order , except as otherwise provided in this chapter :
(1) To his children and their descendants ; if there are
none , then
(2) To his father and mother , if both are living, one
moiety each; but if the father is dead , the mother , if living,
shall take the whole estate ; if the mother is dead , the whole es-
tate shall pass to the father ; if there is no father or mother , then
(3) To his brothers and sisters and their descendants ; if
there are none , then
(4) To the husband or wife of the intestate; if there are
none surviving , then
(5) One moiety of the estate shall pass to the paternal
and the other to the maternal kindred , in the following order :
(a) The grandfather and grandmother equally , if both are
living; but if one is dead , the entire moiety shall go to the
survivor ; if there is no grandfather or grandmother , then
(b) To the uncles and aunts and their descendants ; if there
are none , then
(c) To the great-grandfathers and great-grandmothers , in
the same manner prescribed for grandfather and grandmother
by subsection (a) ; if there are none , then
(d) To the brothers and sisters of the grandfathers and
grandmothers and their descendants; and so on in other cases
without end , passing to the nearest lineal ancestors and their
descendants .
(6) If there is no such kindred to one of the parents as is
described in subsection (5), the whole to go to the kindred of
the other . If there is neither paternal nor maternal kindred , the
whole shall go to the kindred of the husband or wife, as if he
or she had survived the intestate and died entitled to the
estate .
Approved February 27 , 1956

CHAPTER 137
(Н. В. 294)
AN ACT relating to convicts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 432.500 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Any person who interferes with or interrupts the work of
any convict who has been sent to any state penal institution and
is employed outside the walls of the institution , or who gives
or attempts to give , or sells or attempts to sell, to any convict
whether he be employed outside the walls of the institution or
confined within, any opium , cocaine or other narcotic or any
intoxicating liquor , or firearm , weapon or explosive , except ex-
plosives used in line of duty , shall be confined in the penitentiary
for not less than one nor more than five years . Any officer or
guard of any state prison or any superintendent of any work
being carried on by such convicts may arrest , without a warrant ,
any person violating this section . But, this section shall not
prevent any physician in the practice of his profession from
prescribing and delivering to any convict opium , cocaine or
other narcotic , or intoxicating liquor, when he considers it
necessary , if it is furnished with the consent of the person having
charge of the convict .
Approved February 27 , 1956

classification.title: AN ACT concerning the economic and social welfare of individuals, including needy, neglected, dependent and delinquent children, and the organization, functions and duties of certain governmental units and agencies with respect thereto.
classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky Revised Statutes Section 195.020 and 195.030 to consolidate various state functions related to public assistance, employment services, and child welfare under a new Department of Economic Security. It outlines the department's responsibilities in administering unemployment insurance, employment offices, public assistance programs (including general relief, old age assistance, aid to dependent children, and aid to the blind), and services for needy, neglected, dependent, and delinquent children. The act also details the internal structure of the department, including a Bureau of Employment Security with divisions for Unemployment Insurance and Employment Service.

196.030 FUNCTIONS OF DEPARTMENT .

law , exercise all functions of the state in relation to :
( a ) Management of penal , reform and correctional in-
stitutions ;

persons ;
( d ) Supervision of probation and parole ;

to the department ;
( g ) Operating a laboratory of criminal identification and
statistics;
( h ) The giving of assistance to other departments , agen-
cies and institutions of the state and federal government when
requested by performing services in conformity with this sec-
tion;
(i) Acting as the agent of the federal government in mat-
ters of mutual concern , and in the administration of any federal
funds granted to the state to aid in the performance of any
function of this department .
( 2 ) The provisions of this section shall not apply to any
institution , home or agency which does not receive aid from the

classification.summary: This statute details the transfer of appropriations and allocations to the Department of Economic Security and outlines the department's functions, including the management of penal, reform, and correctional institutions, state institutions for children and feeble-minded persons, probation and parole supervision, juvenile probation, a laboratory of criminal identification and statistics, and acting as an agent for the federal government. It specifies that these provisions do not apply to institutions not receiving state, county, or municipal aid.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the administrative and functional organization of state departments, specifically the Department of Economic Security and its responsibilities related to various state institutions and services. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial segregation or discrimination. The statute's focus is on the transfer of funds and the scope of departmental duties, which are administrative in nature and do not appear to have a racial dimension. Therefore, it is classified as not a Jim Crow law.

196.040 DIVISIONS OF DEPARTMENT .
( 1) The department shall be divided into :

( b ) A Division of Probation and Parole , headed by a

( c ) A Division of Children's Institutions , headed by

( 2 ) The divisions shall be under the direction of the

Section 8. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

The Commissioner of the Department of Welfare shall ex-
cept as otherwise provided in Senate Bill No. 205 of the 1956
Regular Session have the power and authority to adopt , amend or
rescind such rules and regulations as he deems necessary or suit-
able for the proper administration of the functions of the depart-
ment or any division therein , including qualification for the re-
ceipt of federal funds and for cooperation with other state and
federal agencies. The commissioner is authorized , subject to the
provisions of KRS Chapters 12 , 42 and 45, to appoint , fix the com-
pensation and prescribe the duties and powers of such officers and
employees as may be necessary in the performance of his duties .
The commissioner may delegate to any such person appointed
such power and authority as he deems reasonable and proper for
the effective administration of the department .

classification.title: Section 199.520 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
classification.summary: This statute outlines the requirements and procedures for finalizing an adoption judgment. It details the court's findings necessary for granting adoption, including the establishment of facts in the petition, compliance with legal requirements, the character and ability of the petitioners, and the best interest of the child. The judgment will change the child's name and will not reference the child's former name or birth parents' names. Upon judgment, the adopted child is legally considered the natural, legitimate child of the adoptive parents for all purposes, except that the child will have no legal relationship to their birth parents regarding personal or property rights, unless a natural parent is the spouse of an adoptive parent. The clerk is required to notify the department of any court action regarding adoption judgments.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text pertains to adoption proceedings in Kentucky. It details the legal requirements for granting an adoption, the effects of adoption on the child's legal status, and the confidentiality of the child's former name and birth parents' names. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. The statute is a civil law concerning family matters and does not fall under the typical categories of Jim Crow laws which enforced racial segregation and discrimination. Therefore, it is classified as 'no' for Jim Crow, with a high confidence score, and the category is null.

classification.title: ADOPTION RECORDS CONFIDENTIAL ; NEW BIRTH CERTIFICATE
classification.summary: This statute outlines the confidentiality of adoption records and the process for creating a new birth certificate for an adopted child. It specifies that court files and records during adoption proceedings are only accessible to parties involved, their attorneys, and departmental representatives, or by court order. Upon finalization, records are sealed. A new birth certificate is to be created by the Division of Vital Statistics, which will be filed with the original, and the original will be stamped as confidential. The new certificate will reflect the child's new name and adoptive parents' names, and if the child is under eighteen, it will not reveal the adoption.

There is no mention of race, nor any provisions that would implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'codes' as it refers to 'Kentucky Revised Statutes' and is an amendment to an existing section (199.570).

classification.title: Section 21. Section 200.220 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is hereby amended and re-enacted to read as follows: 200.220 COUNTY BUREAU TO KEEP CONFIDENTIAL RECORDS.
classification.summary: This statute amends KRS 200.220, concerning county children's bureaus. It mandates that these bureaus keep confidential records of applicants and recipients of assistance, including investigation reports and correspondence. The names of beneficiaries or their mothers are not to be published. Records of the bureau's proceedings and transactions are to be kept with county archives and are open for inspection by members of the fiscal court and the Department of Welfare or its representatives. It also defines 'Department' as the Department of Welfare, 'Child' as a person under eighteen, 'Court' as the juvenile session of the county court, and 'Child welfare worker' as an employee of the Department of Economic Security.
classification.reasoning: The statute pertains to the record-keeping and operational procedures of county children's bureaus and defines terms related to child welfare services. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial segregation or discrimination. The focus is on the confidentiality and accessibility of records for administrative purposes. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'codes' as it refers to amendments to the Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS).

classification.title: Section 23. Section 208.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is hereby amended and re-enacted to read as follows: (1) The county court of each county shall have exclusive jurisdiction in proceedings concerning any child living or found within the county that has committed a public offense. (2) Nothing in KRS 208.010 to 208.590 shall deprive other courts of the right to determine the custody of children upon writs of habeas corpus, or to determine the custody or guardianship of children when such custody or guardianship is incidental to the determination of other causes pending in such other courts; nor shall anything in KRS 208.010 to 208.590 affect the jurisdiction of circuit courts over adoptions and over proceedings for termination of parental rights. Section 24. Section 208.130 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is hereby amended and re-enacted to read as follows: 208.130 DETENTION FACILITIES. (1) The fiscal court of each county shall provide for a suitable facility or facilities for the detention of children held in custody pending disposition of their cases by the juvenile court, and may employ necessary personnel for the operation thereof. (2) In counties containing a city of the first or second class, the fiscal court shall maintain a permanent detention home, either through arrangement with another public agency or with a private organization or agency, or by acquiring and operating the home as a county institution. (3) In counties other than those containing a city of the first or second class, the fiscal court shall either maintain a detention home or arrange for the use of private or family homes. (4) The department may assist the counties in the acquisition, development and furnishing of detention facilities, and may employ available funds for that purpose. The detention facilities may be subject to the supervision of the department, and shall conform to rules and regulations prescribed by the department. (5) Each detention facility shall be conducted under the jurisdiction of the juvenile court for which it is maintained and
classification.summary: This statute amends sections 208.020 and 208.130 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes. Section 208.020 clarifies the exclusive jurisdiction of county courts over children who have committed public offenses, while also preserving the jurisdiction of other courts in matters of child custody and guardianship incidental to other cases, and circuit courts for adoptions and termination of parental rights. Section 208.130 outlines the requirements for county fiscal courts to provide suitable detention facilities for children awaiting juvenile court disposition, with specific provisions for counties containing first or second-class cities and other counties. It also details the department's role in assisting with facility acquisition and supervision.
classification.reasoning: The statute deals with the jurisdiction of county courts over children who have committed public offenses and the provision of detention facilities for juveniles. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial segregation or discrimination. The focus is on the legal and administrative framework for juvenile justice. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

Section 25. Section 208.140 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and re-enacted to read as follows :
208.140 INVESTIGATION BEFORE DISPOSITION OF
CASE .
( 1 ) Before making disposition of the case of a child
brought before the juvenile court , whether by petition pursuant
to KRS 208.070 or by reason of having been taken into custody
pursuant to KRS 208.110 , the judge shall cause an investigation
to be made , concerning the nature of the specific act complained
of, and any circumstances surrounding the child which throw
light on the future care and guidance which should be given the
child. The investigation shall include an inquiry into the child's
age , habits , school record , general reputation and everything that
may pertain to his life and character . The investigation shall also
include an inquiry into the home conditions , life and character
of the person having custody of the child. The result of the
investigation shall be reported in writing to the judge previous
to the final hearing of the child's case , and shall become a part
of the record of the proceedings .
( 2) The investigation shall be conducted by volunteer or
salaried probation officers of the juvenile court or by a suitable
public or private agency. The Department of Economic Security
may furnish investigation services under agreements with the
individual juvenile courts . For this purpose , any county judge
may enter into a contract on behalf of his county with the De-
partment of Economic Security for the furnishing of such
services .

classification.title: 208.180 DISPOSITION OF CHILD CONVICTED OF FELONY; OR MISDEMEANOR
classification.summary: This statute outlines the disposition of a child convicted of a felony in circuit court. If sentenced to imprisonment, the child is committed to the penitentiary. However, for children under twenty years of age at sentencing, the court has the discretion to commit them to the department. If the sentence would expire before the child reaches twenty-one, the commitment to the department is for an indeterminate period.
classification.reasoning: The statute deals with the disposition of children convicted of felonies and misdemeanors. It outlines procedures for transferring cases from juvenile court to circuit court and the subsequent sentencing and commitment of the child. There is no mention of race or any differential treatment based on race. The statute appears to be a general provision of juvenile justice law. The document type is 'codes' as it refers to 'Kentucky Revised Statutes' (KRS) and is presented as an amendment to an existing section of the statutes.

classification.title: ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 1956
classification.summary: This section of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1956 details amendments and re-enactments of Kentucky Revised Statutes related to juvenile courts and the placement of children. It covers provisions for committing children to institutions or agencies, the modification of court orders, and the non-conviction status of juvenile adjudications. It also outlines the Department of Economic Security's role in assisting juvenile courts with placing children in family or boarding homes and the financial responsibilities of counties. A specific section (208.260) mandates that female children transported to institutions must be accompanied by a woman attendant or relative.

Section 31. Section 208.270 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

208.270 COMMITMENT ORDER AND INFORMATION
TO BE FURNISHED DEPARTMENT .
Whenever any court commits a child to the department or
whenever a juvenile court commits a child to any institution or
agency , the court shall cause to be transmitted to the department ,
institution or agency a certified copy of the commitment order ,
together with a summary of the court's information concerning
the child . The child shall be held by virtue of the order , and the
certified copy of the order shall be proof of the authority of the
department , institution or agency in behalf of the child.

ABLE . If it appears that the parent of any child who has been

clerk to be disbursed as ordered by the court or be paid to the

classification.title: AMENDMENTS TO KENTUCKY REVISED STATUTES RELATING TO JUVENILE COURT REPORTS AND PROBATION OFFICERS
classification.summary: This statute amends sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS) concerning juvenile courts. Specifically, it modifies KRS 208.290 regarding annual reports by county clerks to the department on children who have committed public offenses, KRS 208.300 on reports by persons in charge of homes or institutions to the juvenile court and department, and KRS 208.320 concerning the appointment and salaries of chief probation officers and assistants in counties with first or second-class cities.
classification.reasoning: The provided text consists of amendments to existing Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS) related to the juvenile court system. The amendments focus on reporting requirements for county clerks and institutions housing children who have committed public offenses, as well as the appointment and compensation of probation officers. CHAPTER 157 305
treasury . The officers shall serve at the pleasure of the county
judge . The officers shall have the powers of a sheriff in making
arrests .
(2 ) In any county , the county judge may appoint or desig-
nate one or more discreet persons of good moral character to
serve as volunteer probation officers of the juvenile court . Such
officers shall serve during the pleasure of the judge and without
compensation , except that the fiscal court may authorize the
payment of compensation out of the county treasury to any such
officers . Representative of the Department of Economic Se-
curity may be appointed or designated as such volunteer pro-
bation officers , but without compensation from county funds .
( 3) If the Department of Economic Security deems it nec-
essary , it may appoint a child welfare worker to cooperate with
the court .
Section 36. Section 208.330 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and re-enacted to read as follows :
208.330 DUTIES OF PROBATION OFFICERS . Proba-
tion officers of the juvenile court shall :
( 1 ) Make investigations as provided in KRS 208.140 .
(2 ) Be present in court to represent the interest of the
child when the cases investigated by them are heard ; furnish
such information as may be required ; advise with the court as
to the proper disposition of the case ; and take such charge of the
child before and after the hearing as may be ordered .
(3 ) Visit and supervise children placed on probation , and
as far as practicable , aid and encourage such children , by friendly
advice and admonition , to keep the terms of their probation ,
and provide for their rehabilitation .
(4) Make such reports and records as may be required by
the court .
(5 ) Supervise the transfer of children to and from homes
or institutions , as directed by the court .
(6 ) Work in cooperation with the representatives of the
department and of the Department of Economic Security .
Section 37. Section 208.340 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and re- enacted to read as follows :
208.340 INFORMATION OBTAINED BY PROBATION
OFFICERS CONFIDENTIAL .

Section 38. Section 208.400 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and re-enacted to read as follows :
208.400 DUTIES OF DEPARTMENT CONCERNING
EXISTING INSTITUTIONS AND DEVELOPMENT OF AD-
DITIONAL FACILITIES . The department shall be responsible
for the operation , management and development of the existing
state institutional physical facilities for the custodial care or
reformation of children , but not including educational institutions
or institutions designed primarily for the care or treatment of
mentally or physically handicapped children . The department
shall further be responsible for the development of such addi-
tional institutional physical facilities and other necessary physical
facilities , such as forestry camps , farms , or vocational training
facilities , as are necessary to provide an adequate and modern
program for the care, treatment and rehabilitation of children
committed to the state under the juvenile court laws , provided ,
however , that the Kentucky Children's Home shall be used only
for the children that have been committed to the care and
supervision of the Department of Economic Security .
Section 39. Section 208.410 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and re-enacted to read as follows :
208.410 PROGRAM OF CARE , TREATMENT AND RE-
HABILITATION OF CHILDREN TO DEPARTMENT . The
department shall arrange for a program of care , treatment and
rehabilitation of the children committed to it, which program
shall be designed to provide for classification , segregation and
specialized treatment of children according to their respective

Section 40. Section 208.420 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and re -enacted to read as follows :
208.420 RECEPTION CENTER . The department may
establish and operate a reception center for the observation , study
and classification of children committed to it. Such children
shall upon their commitment be placed in the reception center
for a period of observation and study . The Department of Eco-
nomic Security shall furnish the reception center with adequate
psychiatric , psychological and social services. A case history
for each child shall be prepared during his period of observation
and study in the reception center . Upon the basis of study and
observation in the reception center , the Department of Economic
Security shall make a recommendation as to the character of treat-
ment or disposition which should initially be provided for the
child.
Section 41. Section 208.430 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and re -enacted to read as follows :
208.430 POWERS OF DEPARTMENT DURING PE-
RIOD OF COMMITMENT .
(1 ) Any child committed to the department who has com-
mitted a public offense may , on the recommendation of the child
welfare worker , at any time during the period of his commit-
ment , be :
( a ) Discharged from commitment , unless he was com-
mitted by a circuit court upon a felony conviction and was given
a sentence the period of which will extend beyond the age of
twenty -one;
(b ) Placed on parole in the home of his parents , or in a
suitable foster home , family home or boarding home , upon such
conditions as the department may prescribe and subject to
visitation and supervision by a child welfare worker ;
(c) Placed in one of the institutions operated by the de-
partment ; except the Kentucky Children's Home ;
(d) Placed in a child -caring institution operated by a local
governmental unit or by a private organization willing to receive
the child, upon such conditions as the department may prescribe ;
( e) Disposed of as provided in KRS 208.460 or 208.470 .

Section 45. Section 208.470 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and re-enacted to read as follows :
208.470 DISPOSITION OF CHILD INCAPABLE OF
BENEFITTING FROM TREATMENT BY DEPARTMENT
FACILITIES . If it appears to the department that a child
committed to it by reason of the commission of a public offense
constituting a felony , is incapable of benefitting from treatment
by the facilities of the department , the child shall be returned by
the department to the circuit court of the county in which the
offense was committed , for appropriate proceedings . If the child
originally was committed to the department by such circuit court ,
following a conviction of the felony upon a trial in the circuit
court , the court shall commit the child to the state penitentiary
for the duration of the sentence fixed by the verdict , allowing
credit for such period of time as the child was in custody of the
department . Otherwise , the circuit court shall proceed the same
as if the child was an adult under arrest on a felony charge ,
and in the event the child is convicted and sentenced on the
felony charge , credit shall be allowed on the sentence for the
period of time the child was in the custody of the department .

208.550 METHOD OF ACQUIRING LANDS AND
BUILDINGS . Acquisition of lands and buildings for the de-
partment shall be in accordance with the provisions of KRS

Section 53. ( 1 ) The Commissioner of Economic Security
shall have sole charge of the administration of the Division of
Children's Services .
( 2) The Commissioner of Economic Security shall have
the power and authority to adopt , amend or rescind such rules
and regulations as he deems necessary or suitable for the proper
administration of the functions of the Division of Children's
Services, including qualification for the receipt of federal funds
and for cooperation with other state and federal agencies . Such
rules and regulations shall become effective in the manner and at
the time prescribed by the Commissioner of Economic Security .
( 3 ) The Commissioner of Economic Security is author-
ized , subject to the provisions of KRS Chapters 12, 42, and 45,
to appoint , fix the compensation and prescribe the duties and
powers of such officers and employees as may be necessary in the
performance of his duties under the provisions of the law relating
to the Division of Children's Services . All positions shall be
filled by persons selected and appointed on a non - partisan merit
basis , in accordance with merit standards established by law.
The Commissioner of Economic Security shall not employ or pay
any person who is an officer or committee member of any po-
litical party organization . The Commissioner of Economic Se-
curity may delegate to any such person so appointed such power
and authority as he deems reasonable and proper for the effec-
tive administration of the Division of Children's Services .
Section 54. Sections 208.210 , 208.500 , 208.560 , 208.570 ,
208.580 , 208.590 and 208.595 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are hereby repealed .
Section 55. An emergency is declared to exist and the rea-
son for the emergency is to facilitate and provide for the econ-
omy , efficiency and improved administration of services relating
to needy , neglected , dependent and delinquent children and
adoptions . The Act shall become law upon the approval of the
Governor.
Approved February 27 , 1956

classification.title: AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation.
classification.summary: This act requires life insurance companies doing business in Kentucky to report to the Department of Revenue by February 15th each year. The report must include names and addresses of Kentucky residents entitled to proceeds of life insurance policies left on deposit, along with the amounts. It also requires reporting on beneficiaries of policies subject to taxation under KRS 132.215, including amounts paid in the preceding twelve months, the age of the recipient, and other information as required by the Department.

classification.title: Amendment to KRS 137.040 regarding license taxes
classification.summary: This statute amends Section 137.040 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning license taxes. It clarifies definitions for 'Department', 'License stamp', 'Person', and 'Itinerant operator'. The amendment states that no person shall engage in certain occupations or sell certain articles without paying the prescribed license tax and obtaining a license. However, it exempts itinerant operators from paying a license tax in a county if they have already paid it in the county where they first engaged in the occupation. It also exempts county or community fair associations, corporations, or similar enterprises, and their agents or concessionaires, from this tax when operating within the grounds of a festival or fair.

classification.title: An Act to Amend KRS 137.050 relating to license fees for possession and sale of cigarettes and for businesses serving meals, soft drinks, ice cream, and theaters.
classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS) 137.050 to adjust license fees. It imposes a $25 annual license fee for possessing or selling cigarettes without state tax stamps, or for selling tax-paid cigarettes for resale within the state. It also sets annual license fees for restaurants ($10), retail outlets selling soft drinks or ice cream ($5, or $10 if both are sold), and theaters ($0.20 per seat, with a minimum of $10). For drive-in theaters, each parking space is equivalent to two seats. Exemptions are provided for chautauquas, fairs, and buildings owned by religious, educational, or charitable organizations for non-commercial exhibitions. The act clarifies that 'possessing' does not include interstate transportation or temporary holding by transportation agencies.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns license fees for various businesses, including those selling cigarettes, food, and operating theaters. There is no mention of race, nor any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. The statute appears to be purely economic regulation. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as it is an 'ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY' from 1956, which indicates it was passed during a legislative session. The category is 'other' as it does not fit into the specific categories provided for Jim Crow laws.

AN ACT relating to amusement taxes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Any person who sells admission to places of amusement
or entertainment without first procuring the permit required by
KRS 138.040 shall pay, in addition to the tax imposed by KRS
138.020 , a penalty equal to twenty -five percent of the amount
of the tax ; provided , however , that the penalty may be reduced
to five percent if the Department of Revenue is convinced that
failure to procure the permit was not a willful violation of KRS
138.040 . This penalty shall be in addition to penalties provided
by KRS 138.030 and the fine provided by KRS 138.990 ( 1) .

has induced or coerced , any automobile dealer to enter into
any agreement with such manufacturer , factory branch or
representative thereof , or to do any other act unfair to said
dealer , by threatening to cancel any franchise existing between
such manufacturer , factory branch or representative thereof
and said dealer ; or
(n) Being a manufacturer , factory branch , distributor ,
field representative , officer , agent or any representative what-
soever of such motor vehicle manufacturer or factory branch ,
who has unfairly , without due regard to the equities of said
dealer and without just provocation , canceled the franchise of
any motor vehicle dealer . The nonrenewal of a franchise or
selling agreement without just provocation or cause shall be
deemed an evasion of this section and shall constitute an unfair
cancellation .
( 2) The licensor may without notice deny the applica-
tion for a license within 30 days after receipt thereof by written
notice to the applicant , stating the grounds for such denial .
Upon request by the applicant , whose license has been so
denied , the licensor shall set the time and place of hearing a
review of such denial , the same to be heard with reasonable
promptness .
(3) No license shall be suspended or revoked except
after a hearing thereon . The licensor shall give the licensee
at least 30 days notice of the time and place of hearing and
of the charges against it .
(4) The department may inspect the pertinent books ,
letters , records and contracts of a licensee .

classification.title: ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
classification.summary: This statute addresses unfair trade practices related to the financing of motor vehicle retail sales contracts. It prohibits manufacturers, wholesalers, or distributors from coercing dealers into selling or assigning their retail sales contracts to specific sales finance companies through inducements, promises, or threats. It also prohibits manufacturers from making payments or providing services to sales finance companies if it lessens competition or creates an unfair trade advantage. Dealers who are coerced into such arrangements are not considered guilty of unlawful acts and may be compelled to testify.

It focuses on preventing unfair trade practices and monopolies within the automotive sales industry. The document type is identified as 'session_laws' based on the header 'ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY' and the year '1956'.

or provision hereof shall be declared invalid , the decision of

maining parts or provisions of the Act .

missioner .
(b ) In the discretion of the commissioner , an applicant
whose license to do business or act as a life insurance agent in
this state was suspended less than one year prior to the date of
application .
( 2 ) The commissioner shall establish rules and regulations
with respect to the scope, type and conduct of such written
examinations and the times and places within this state where
they shall be held. Applicants shall be permitted to take such
examinations on any business day at the principal office of the
commissioner .
(3 ) No person who shall have taken and failed to pass
two examinations given pursuant to this section shall be entitled
to take any further examination until after the expiration of
six months from the date of the last examination in which he
failed. If such person shall thereafter fail to pass two more
such examinations he shall not be eligible to take any further
examination until after the expiration of one year from the date
of his last unsuccessful examination . An examination fee shall
be paid for each and every examination .

classification.title: AN ACT relating to vocational education.
classification.summary: This act repeals and re-enacts sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes related to vocational education. It formally accepts and agrees to comply with federal acts concerning vocational education, aiming to provide training and skill development for young people in regular day schools, as well as out-of-school youth and adults. The purpose is to equip workers with the necessary skills and knowledge for their chosen vocations.
classification.reasoning: The statute pertains to vocational education and its acceptance of federal guidelines. There is no mention of race, segregation, or any discriminatory practices. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The confidence is high because the subject matter is clearly vocational education and lacks any racial component. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by 'ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY' and 'CHAPTER 165'.

classification.title: CHAPTER 165
classification.summary: This statute repeals and re-enacts sections 163.040 and 163.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, concerning the administration of vocational education. It vests the State Board of Education with authority to carry out vocational education programs, accept and expend funds, and outlines the supervisory and appointment powers of the Superintendent of Public Instruction. The State Board of Education is empowered to make rules and regulations for effective implementation.

classification.title: AN ACT relating to Social Security for public employes , amending section 61.430 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , and declaring an emergency to exist .
classification.summary: This act amends Section 61.430 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes to authorize the state agency, with the Governor's approval, to enter into an agreement with the Federal Security Administrator to extend Social Security benefits (Old-Age and Survivors Insurance) to employees of the Commonwealth or its political subdivisions. The agreement will cover provisions related to coverage, benefits, contributions, effective date, modification, termination, and administration, ensuring benefits are provided on the same basis as if services constituted employment under Title II of the Social Security Act. The state will pay contributions to the Secretary of the Treasury.

classification.title: CHAPTER 168
classification.summary: This statute outlines the requirements for obtaining and maintaining permits for operating centers. It specifies that permits are issued by a superintendent, are non-transferable, and have a maximum duration of three years. Existing centers must apply for a permit within sixty days of the act's effective date. Application forms require details about the center's personnel and certificates of compliance from local officials (health officer, fire marshal, building inspector). If a permit is not issued within three months, the center must cease operations. Initial permit fees are thirty-five dollars, with renewals costing ten dollars. Permits expire one year from their effective date and must be posted conspicuously. Changes in address require the superintendent's approval.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text details the licensing and operational requirements for 'centers' in Kentucky. It focuses on administrative procedures, application processes, fees, and compliance with health and safety regulations. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial segregation or discrimination. The requirements appear to apply universally to any entity operating as a 'center' as defined by the act. Therefore, it is classified as not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 170
(Н. В. 478)
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 136.190 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is here-
by amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The superintendent of schools in each district in which
any individual , group of individuals or corporation , operates
public utility or franchise taxpaying property assessed under
KRS 136.120 shall, on or before the first day of January, 1957 ,
furnish to the county clerk of the county in which the district is
situated and to the Department of Revenue the boundary of his
school district . The superintendent of school in each district in
which any franchise -paying corporation , individual , or group
of individuals operates shall , on or before the first day of Janu-
ary , 1958 , and each year thereafter , furnish to the county clerk
and to the Department of Revenue any changes made in the
boundary of his school district during the immediate preceding
twelve months .
(2 ) The engineer of cities of the first class and the city
clerk of cities of the second, third, fourth , fifth , and sixth classes
shall notify the county clerk and the Department of Revenue of
their boundaries in the same manner as required of the superin-
tendent of schools in subsection ( 1 ) .
( 3 ) The responsible governing official or the chairman
of the governing body of any taxing district other than the
county , school district , or city shall notify the county clerk and
the Department of Revenue of their boundaries in the same
manner as required of the superintendent of schools in subsection
(1) .
Approved February 27, 1956

As used in KRS 138.650 to 138.725 and 138.990 ( 21 ) and
(22 ) unless the context requires otherwise :
( 1 ) "Department " means Department of Revenue .
( 2 ) " Certificate " means the certificate of public conveni-
ence and necessity as defined in KRS 281.010 ( 3 ) .
( 3 ) "Person " includes every natural person , fiduciary , as-
sociation , state or political subdivision , or corporation . Whenever
used in any clause describing and imposing imprisonment the
term " person " as applied to an association means and includes
the partners or members thereof , and as applied to a corpora-
tion the officers thereof .
(4) "Public highway " means every way or place generally
open to the use of the public for the purpose of vehicular travel
notwithstanding that it may be temporarily closed or travel
thereon restricted for the purpose of maintenance repair or re-
construction .
( 5) “Motor carrier " means every person who operates or
causes to be operated on any highway in this state any motor
vehicle , trailer , or semi -trailer , of a gross weight of not less than
18,001 pounds , engaged in the transportation of persons or prop-
erty in either private or for-hire carriage in interstate commerce
in this state , or who purchases fuel without this state for con-
sumption in this state in intrastate commerce . The term "motor
carrier " shall include every common and contract carrier pos-
sessing authority as such from the Interstate Commerce Com-
mission together with those engaged in transporting in inter-
state commerce in this state commodities not regulated by the
Interstate Commerce Commission . The term "motor carrier "
shall not mean or shall not include any person operating or
causing to be operated a city bus except city buses operated
across the border of the state of Kentucky .
